title: Latest News from Science Magazine
description: Keyword search result for Alerts: News Weekly Headlines -- New results matching your keyword search query (updated daily).

items.title: Mathematicians issue warning as AI rapidly gains ground
items.description: New declaration argues the technology jeopardizes the field’s values and culture

items.title: Doubling down on controversial claims, Microsoft accelerates quantum computing plans
items.description: Company says its improved topological qubits will lead to a practical machine in 3 years—as critics scoff

items.title: How Europe hopes to lure foreign researchers with massive new grants
items.description: Maria Leptin, president of the European Research Council, on supergrants, AI-generated submissions, and the agency’s future

items.title: Radical proposal would block solar storms with orbital ‘airbag’
items.description: Gases released from satellites could slash the threat of severe space weather

items.title: Exclusive: NSF watchdog unit is no longer investigating research misconduct
items.description: New policy hands universities the reins in probing allegations against faculty

items.title: Brain mapping effort searches for roots of Parkinson’s and other diseases
items.description: Allen Institute initiative aims to capture earliest cellular changes and identify new drug targets

items.title: Desperate to fight Ebola outbreak, Congo weighs using longshot vaccine options
items.description: Existing vaccines may offer some protection against the rare strain now circulating—but the evidence is scant

items.title: Court blocks NSF’s transfer of climate lab’s supercomputing facility
items.description: Proposed switch in management caused irreparable harm to NCAR, judge finds

items.title: Exclusive: HHS is now weighing in on science in NIH grants
items.description: Staffers say comments coming after NIH’s own approvals are overriding peer review

items.title: ‘That could be a game changer’: antiviral pill gets first test for Ebola prevention
items.description: Researchers are preparing to launch an unprecedented trial to protect people exposed to Ebola in the DRC and Uganda
